data.title: Cautious Long-Term Outlook For Natural Diamond Prices — Market Talk
data.description:
1731 ET - The long-term outlook for natural diamond prices remains cautious, Lucara Diamond says, with the market continuing to navigate structural shifts. Near term, premium-grade natural diamonds are showing renewed strength, Lucara adds. However, mid-range and lower-grade stones continue to face pricing pressure from high inventories, cautious consumer sentiment, and a rise of lab-grown diamonds. "Prices of lab-grown diamonds have continued to decrease in 2025 with production outweighing demand," Lucara says. The company also said global natural diamond production is expected to decrease, following significant production guidance cuts by major diamond producers. It added that encouraging sign are emerging in the recovery of the Chinese market. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

1712 ET - Skechers USA is running in the right direction. The shoe company, which is slated to go private, announced 2Q results Friday that showed sales rose 13% to $2.44 billion while earnings rose 22% to $170.5 million. The performance shows the company is humming ahead of its sale to private equity firm 3G Capital, in a $9.4 billion deal announced in May. The results come a day after clog and sandal company Crocs warned sales would tumble in its current quarter, prompting a sharp decline in its stock price. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1655 ET - Underlying signs show Cisco is having a strong current quarter, but investors shouldn't expect the company, which has a new CFO, to raise its outlook, say Raymond James analysts in a research note. The company's routing and optical business is being boosted by high AI traffic and the company's security systems are seeing strong demand, while Cisco's AI business is showing considerable potential. "Buy-side sentiment has improved materially," the analysts say, but note that the company is unlikely to go out on a limb with a raised outlook, especially amid heightened economic uncertainty, which might justify withholding a 2026 forecast. Shares rose 2.7% to $71.79 Friday. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1634 ET - The difficulties that private-equity firms are facing to sell portfolio companies-and monetize performance fees-are creating opportunities for private-credit and other lenders to provide capital not only to buyout shops but also to their principals. Citizens Bank, for one, recently launched a credit line tailored to partners and professionals of private-equity and venture-capital firms. The roughly $220 billion bank, which serves more than 700 such firms, cited "the current market environment, marked by volatility and higher-for-longer rates," that is extending by as many as two years the already long asset-holding periods of private funds. "This means money is taking much longer to return to investors, including the partners and professionals within these firms." (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

1608 ET - Sweetgreen's forward view will have to be materially tempered as it deals with slower traffic and reduced expectations, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a research note. The salad restaurant chain cut its full-year outlook and logged a wider loss in 2Q, with CEO Jonathan Neman noting that the results reflect several headwinds, including macroeconomic pressures. "We don't think macro pressures, which have been visible in much of fast casual lately, explain the headwinds as much as execution in stores," the analysts counter. "Broadly, it seems there are value prop issues, compounded by the macro, some turn-off of frequent customers, and operational/experience issues in stores." Shares dive 23% to $9.74 ahead of the closing bell, its largest percentage decrease on record. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1602 ET - U.S. stocks rise broadly for the week as investors digest latest round of tariffs and increased rate-cut odds as technology shares send Nasdaq back to fresh highs. Gold prices climb to all-time highs before futures give up almost all of those gains after the White House said it would clarify tariffs on the precious metal. Communication services and information technology shares are the best performing sectors as the Nasdaq gains 3.8% for the week. DJIA adds 206 points, or 0.5%, to 44175, the S&P 500 rises 0.8% to 6389 and the Nasdaq climbs 1% to 21450. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

1554 ET - Increased bets on an interest rate cut by the Fed drive a Treasurys selloff that pushes yields higher for the week, ahead of key inflation data due Tuesday. Markets are pricing two or more cuts this year, but a lot depends on upcoming indicators. Economists surveyed by WSJ expect July 12-month CPI to accelerate to 2.8% from June's 2.7%, with the core rising to 3.1% from 2.9%. Analysts say tariffs are boosting some prices, but the impact on inflation could be temporary. The 10-year rises 0.064 percentage point this week, to 4.282% and the two-year adds 0.056 p.p. to 3.757%. Both are up for the day. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1534 ET - Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill slightly missed the company's efficacy expectations, but shares slid more than they should've, say UBS analysts. The pill led to 12.4% weight loss, which suggests it's still a viable drug. Consumers who want an oral GPL-1 drug may be willing to sacrifice some efficacy to avoid needles. The pill version will also cater to those looking to buy in cash, who value convenience over maximum efficacy, the analysts say. But the lower efficacy could lead Eli Lilly to offer the pill at a discount, which would hurt its potential boost to the company's earnings. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1428 ET [Dow Jones]--Doximity's first-quarter earnings beat shows that its added services are drawing physicians to its platform, which is known as the "LinkedIn for doctors," and pharma ad spending could soon follow, says Mizuho analyst Steven Valiquette. The company's newsfeed reached a record number of users while its AI tool utilization multiplied by five. The company is also seeing strong engagement for AI Scribe, a patient note-taking tool it launched last week. Doximity will "attract greater biopharma digital marketing spend as the app is now reaching rapidly higher and broader levels of physician engagement," Valiquette said. The stock was up 12% to $65.30 Friday. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1401 ET - Maplebear, also known as Instacart, can further leverage its position as a top-two player in grocery delivery, say Oppenheimer analysts who remain bullish on the company. The analysts raised the company's price target to $65 from $60, saying it is showing better new customer retention than last year. Instacart, which will likely see slowed orders from UberEats comparable sales in the third quarter, is expected to see gross transaction value similar to the past four quarters, the analysts say. Shares rise 6.7% at $52.70.(natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)

1345 ET [Dow Jones]--The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. edged up by one this week to 411, ending a string of 14 weeks of declines, oil service firm Baker Hughes reports. Oil rigs were down by 74 from a year ago. Natural-gas rigs slipped by one to 123 after adding 16 in the previous three weeks. Bank of America strategists Francisco Blanch and Clifton White attribute the climb in natural-gas rigs, led by the Haynesville region, partly to optimism about demand for LNG exports. "It only took 2026 gas prices trading $4-$4.5/mmBtu year to date to increase the natural-gas rig count by more than 20% since 1Q25," they said in a note. "We expect to see incremental production from these newly added gas rigs just as Golden Pass [LNG] enters service by the end of the year." (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1345 ET - Local governments in the U.S. have been flooding the market with new municipal bonds. The added supply has been blamed for a decline in the price --and consequent increase in yields-- of munis. Painful as they may be for bond holders, high yields are an appealing entry point for investors interested in the munis' tax exemption benefit. Conning's Cindy Beualieu expects munis to remain attractive, but at more normal levels. Issuance, which reached a record $513.6 billion last year and is 18% higher year-to-date from a year earlier, is likely to settle around $200 billion a year, "which is reasonable" she says. "We aren't calling for significantly lower rates." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Talkspace, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Talkspace, Inc., a leading provider of online therapy services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational metrics, reflecting the company's strategic focus and performance in the digital mental health industry.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $54.3 million, increased by 17.9% from $46.1 million in the same period last year, driven by a 35.3% increase in Payor revenue.

Loss from operations: $(1.8) million, improved by 48.7% from a loss of $(3.5) million in the same period last year, reflecting better cost management.

Net loss: $(0.5) million, slightly increased from a net loss of $(0.5) million in the same period last year, indicating stable financial performance.

Net loss per share: $(0.00), consistent with the same period last year, reflecting stable share performance despite operational losses.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Talkspace's revenue is divided into three main segments: Payor, Direct-to-Enterprise (DTE), and Consumer. Payor revenue saw a significant increase due to a higher number of completed sessions, while Consumer revenue declined as the company strategically focused on attracting Payor members.

Sales Units: For the three and six months ended June 30, 2025, Talkspace's clinicians completed approximately 385,100 and 735,100 sessions, respectively, for members covered under Payor customers, indicating a substantial increase in service delivery compared to the previous year.

Customer Segments: The number of health plan customers increased to 31 from 24, while the number of enterprise customers decreased to 165 from 187, reflecting a shift in customer base dynamics.

Consumer Active Members: As of June 30, 2025, the number of Consumer active members decreased to approximately 6,650 from 10,700, aligning with the company's strategic focus on Payor members.

Future Outlook: Talkspace anticipates continued growth in Payor revenue driven by increased session completions and plans to maintain its strategic focus on this segment. The company is also assessing the impact of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act on its operations.

SEC Filing: Talkspace, Inc. [ TALK ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: MEDICAL PROPERTIES TRUST INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Medical Properties Trust Inc., a real estate investment trust specializing in healthcare facilities, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, reflecting both challenges and strategic initiatives undertaken during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $240.4 million, reflecting a decrease from $266.6 million in the same period last year, primarily due to lower rent billed and income from financing leases.
  • Net Loss: $(98.1) million, compared to a net loss of $(319.7) million in the prior year period, indicating an improvement in financial performance.
  • Net Loss Attributable to MPT Common Stockholders: $(98.4) million, compared to $(320.6) million in the previous year, showing a reduction in losses.
  • Net Loss Per Common Share — Basic and Diluted: $(0.16), compared to $(0.54) in the same period last year, indicating a decrease in loss per share.
  • Total Comprehensive Income (Loss): $112.5 million, compared to a comprehensive loss of $(334.7) million in the prior year, driven by foreign currency translation gains.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in multiple regions, with significant assets in the United States, United Kingdom, Switzerland, Germany, and Spain. The United States and United Kingdom represent the largest portions of total assets, each accounting for approximately 50% of the company's total assets.
  • Revenue Segments: The company generates revenue from various facility types, including general acute care hospitals, behavioral health facilities, post-acute care facilities, and freestanding ER/urgent care facilities. General acute care hospitals contribute the largest share of total assets and revenue.
  • Re-tenanting Activity: Following the release of claims on 23 properties, the company reached agreements with six operators to lease 18 facilities. These leases include a rent ramp-up period, with cash rents expected to increase significantly by the end of 2026.
  • New Investments: In 2025, the company invested in the SwissMedical Network real estate joint venture and funded approximately $39 million to Steward Health Care System's secured lender to gain control over certain real estate assets.
  • Development and Capital Addition Activities: The company is actively involved in development projects, including facilities in Spain, Kansas, Idaho, and Arizona. Two other projects in Texas and Massachusetts are ongoing, with costs to complete construction estimated between $50 million and $55 million.
  • Disposals: During the first half of 2025, the company completed the sale of three facilities and an ancillary facility, resulting in a gain on real estate.
  • Prospect Bankruptcy: Prospect's bankruptcy proceedings are ongoing, with a global settlement approved in March 2025. The company has recorded impairment charges and is involved in additional loan advances to support the debtor.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on re-tenanting properties and managing its investments in unconsolidated entities. It continues to monitor tenant performance and adjust its strategies to optimize asset utilization and revenue generation.

SEC Filing: MEDICAL PROPERTIES TRUST INC [ MPW ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Lifetime Brands Is Maintained at Buy by Canaccord Genuity
Immix Biopharma, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Immix Biopharma, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative cell therapies for serious diseases, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and key business developments, highlighting significant progress in its clinical programs and strategic initiatives.

Financial Highlights

Net Loss: $6.6 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $4.4 million for the three months ended June 30, 2024, primarily due to increased research and development expenses.

Net Loss: $11.2 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $9.7 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, driven by higher general and administrative expenses and research and development costs.

Loss Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.22) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(0.15) for the three months ended June 30, 2024.

Loss Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.37) for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(0.36) for the six months ended June 30, 2024.

Business Highlights

Product Development: Immix Biopharma, Inc. is focused on developing its lead cell therapy candidate, NXC-201, for the treatment of AL Amyloidosis and other serious diseases. NXC-201 is currently being evaluated in ongoing Phase 1b/2 clinical trials in the United States and ex-U.S. regions.

Regulatory Designations: NXC-201 has been awarded Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) Designation by the FDA and Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) by both the FDA and European Commission for AL Amyloidosis, which could potentially streamline the path to FDA approval.

Clinical Trials: As of August 2025, the company has treated 10 relapsed/refractory AL Amyloidosis patients in the U.S. and 16 patients in ex-U.S. clinical trials. The trials are being conducted at multiple sites, including Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center as the lead site.

Grant Funding: The company received an $8 million grant from the California Institute for Regenerative Medicine (CIRM) to support the clinical development of NXC-201. As of August 2025, $3.6 million has been received, with $4.4 million remaining contingent on milestone achievements.

New Production Facilities: In January 2024, Immix Biopharma entered into a long-term operating lease for a biopharmaceutical manufacturing space in California, which is expected to support the production needs for its clinical trials and future commercialization efforts.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue expanding its clinical programs and is seeking additional capital to fund operations beyond the next twelve months. Immix Biopharma is also exploring various financing sources and strategic partnerships to support its growth and development activities.

SEC Filing: Immix Biopharma, Inc. [ IMMX ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: CATHAY GENERAL BANCORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Cathay General Bancorp, a prominent financial institution with a significant presence in various states and international locations, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, providing insights into its profitability, revenue streams, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest and Dividend Income: Cathay General Bancorp reported a total interest and dividend income of $322.9 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025. This represents a decrease from $332.9 million in the same period of 2024, primarily due to lower interest income from loans receivable.

Net Interest Income Before Provision for Credit Losses: The company achieved a net interest income of $181.2 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $165.3 million in the same period of 2024, indicating improved interest margins.

Net Income: Cathay General Bancorp's net income for the three months ended June 30, 2025, was $77.5 million, compared to $66.8 million in the same period of 2024. This increase was driven by higher net interest income and lower interest expense.

Net Income Per Common Share - Basic: The net income per common share (basic) was $1.11 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $0.92 in the same period of 2024, reflecting improved profitability.

Net Income Per Common Share - Diluted: The net income per common share (diluted) was $1.10 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.92 in the same period of 2024, indicating enhanced earnings per share performance.

Business Highlights

Geographical Performance: Cathay General Bancorp operates 24 branches in Southern California, 17 branches in Northern California, 9 branches in New York State, and additional branches in Washington State, Illinois, Texas, Maryland, Massachusetts, Nevada, New Jersey, Hong Kong, and representative offices in Taipei, Beijing, and Shanghai. This extensive network supports the company's operations and customer reach.

Future Outlook: The company expects that the most significant estimate subject to change is the allowance for loan losses, indicating a focus on managing credit risk in the future. Additionally, the company is monitoring economic conditions and their potential impact on loan performance and credit losses.

SEC Filing: CATHAY GENERAL BANCORP [ CATY ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Basis flat steady as futures sink
data.description:
Spot basis bids for hard red winter wheat were unchanged in a spot check of locations in the southern U.S. Plains on Friday as futures fell.

  • Wheat futures were down on abundant global supplies, but received some support as low prices sparked export interest.

  • K.C. September hard red winter wheat (KWU25) settled 3-1/4 cents lower at $5.18-1/4 a bushel.

  • Protein premiums for railcar wheat shipped to and through Kansas City gained 15 cents for wheat with 11% to 11.6% protein content, 5 cents for 12% to 12.6%, and 10 cents for 12.8%, 13% and 13.6%. It was unchanged for all other grades of wheat.

data.title: Urgent.ly CFO Michael Port Exits Company
data.description:
By Natalie Weger

Urgent.ly said its Michael Port, its chief financial officer, left the digital roadside assistance platform company on Tuesday.

Port left the "without case" as defined under the terms of his promotion letter, the company said in a Securities and Exchange Commission filing on Friday.

The company said it named Chief Executive Matthew Booth as principal financial officer in addition to his current role. Andrea Makkai, corporate controller of the company, will serve as principal accounting officer.

Write to Natalie Weger at natalie.weger@wsj.com

data.title: Westlake Corporation Declares Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
$0.53 per share dividend declared payable on September 4, 2025

HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 08, 2025--

The Board of Directors of Westlake Corporation today declared a regular dividend distribution of $0.53 per share for the second quarter of 2025. This dividend will be payable on September 4, 2025 to stockholders of record on August 19, 2025.

Westlake announced its first dividend on November 11, 2004 and has successively been paying and increasing its dividend for the past 20 years.

The statements in this release that are not historical facts, including statements regarding future payment of dividend, are forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements are subject to significant risks and uncertainties. For more detailed information about the factors that could cause actual results to differ materially, please refer to Westlake's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, which was filed with the SEC in February 2025, and Westlake's Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, which was filed with the SEC in May 2025, respectively.

About Westlake

Westlake is a global manufacturer and supplier of materials and innovative products that enhance life every day. Headquartered in Houston, with operations in Asia, Europe and North America, we provide the building blocks for vital solutions — from housing and construction, to packaging and healthcare, to automotive and consumer. For more information, visit the Company's web site at www.westlake.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250808222793/en/

CONTACT: Media Inquiries:

Westlake Corp.

Ben Ederington, 713-960-9111

or

Investor Inquiries:

Westlake Corp.

Steve Bender, 713-960-9111

data.title: Heard on the Street Recap: Goldfinger — WSJ
data.description:
By Heard Editors

What Happened in Markets Today

Gold markets were thrown into chaos by tariff confusion. Gold prices briefly soared to new highs on the positively shocking news that imports of gold bars from Switzerland would be included in the 39% tariff slapped on the country Thursday. This threatened to irradiate the functioning of gold futures markets, which depend on importing bars to settle contracts. But prices fell back in the afternoon after a White House official said President Trump would soon issue an executive order "clarifying misinformation about the tariffing of gold bars."

Trump will appoint adviser Stephen Miran to the Federal Reserve. Miran, currently the head of the White House Council of Economic Advisers, will fill a short-term vacancy through January. He has been a frequent, outspoken critic of the Fed. He has lambasted Fed policymakers for what he calls "tariff derangement syndrome," and argues there is so far no sign that tariffs will cause inflation. In a paper last year, he argued that all of the Fed's top officials should be subject to at-will dismissal by the White House.

Meanwhile, Trump's search has widened for the next Fed chair. The Wall Street Journal reported Friday that the president is considering a list of around 10 people. This includes the usual suspects: National Economic Council director Kevin Hassett; Fed governor Christopher Waller; and former Fed governor Kevin Warsh. But it also includes two new names: Former St. Louis Fed President James Bullard and Marc Sumerlin, a former economic adviser to President George W. Bush.

Stocks continued their ascent. Bears played their golden harps as markets charged ahead. The Dow Jones Industrials Average gained 207 points, or 0.5%. The Nasdaq rose nearly 1% to a new closing high.

This analysis comes from the Journal's Heard on the Street team. Subscribe to their free daily afternoon newsletter here.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: D. Boral Capital Acted as Lead Bookrunner to Robot Consulting Co., Ltd (Nasdaq:LAWR) in Connection with its $15,000,000 Initial Public Offering
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / On July 18, 2025, Robot Consulting Co., Ltd. (the "Company" or "Robot Consulting"), a Japanese platform service provider focusing on human resource solutions with an intention to expand into legal technology and the metaverse, announced the closing of its initial public offering (the "Offering") of 3,750,000 American Depositary Shares ("ADSs") at a public offering price of US$4 per ADS. Each ADS represents one ordinary share of the Company. The ADSs began trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on July 17, 2025, under the ticker symbol "LAWR."

The Company received aggregate gross proceeds of $15,000,000 from the Offering, before deducting underwriting discounts and other related expenses. In addition, the Company has granted the underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 562,500 ADSs at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts.

The net proceeds from the Offering will be used for: (i) recruiting talent and increasing the number of employees, (ii) investing in the research and development of the Company's new and existing products, (iii) investing in equipment and facilities, including expanding the Company's office to accommodate additional employees, and (iv) working capital and general corporate purposes.

The Offering was conducted on a firm commitment basis. D. Boral Capital LLC acted as the representative of the underwriters, with Craft Capital Management LLC acting as the co-underwriter (collectively, the "Underwriters") for the Offering. Hunter Taubman Fischer & Li LLC acted as U.S. securities counsel to the Company and Winston & Strawn LLP acted as U.S. counsel to the Underwriters in connection with the Offering. Spirit Advisors LLC acted as the financial advisor to the Company.

A registration statement on Form F-1 relating to the Offering was filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") (File Number: 333-284875), as amended, and was declared effective by the SEC on June 30, 2025. The Offering was made only by means of a prospectus, forming a part of the registration statement. Copies of the final prospectus relating to the Offering may be obtained from D. Boral Capital LLC, Attention: Compliance Department, 590 Madison Avenue, New York, NY 10022, via email at info@dboralcapital.com or telephone at +1 (212) 970-5150 or from Craft Capital Management LLC by email at info@craftcm.com, by standard mail to 377 Oak St, Lower Concourse, Garden City, NY 11530, or by telephone at +1 (800) 550-8411. In addition, copies of the final prospectus relating to the Offering may be obtained via the SEC's website at www.sec.gov.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell, or the solicitation of an offer to buy any of the Company's securities, nor shall such securities be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from registration, nor shall there be any offer, solicitation, or sale of any of the Company's securities in any state or jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation, or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of such state or jurisdiction.

About D. Boral Capital

D. Boral Capital LLC is a premier, relationship-driven global investment bank headquartered in New York. The firm is dedicated to delivering exceptional strategic advisory and tailored financial solutions to middle-market and emerging growth companies. With a proven track record, D. Boral Capital provides expert guidance to clients across diverse sectors worldwide, leveraging access to capital from key markets, including the United States, Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and Latin America.

A recognized leader on Wall Street, D. Boral Capital has successfully aggregated approximately $30 billion in capital since its inception in 2020, executing ~350 transactions across a broad range of investment banking products.

Forward Looking Statement

Certain statements in this announcement are forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements involve known and unknown risks and uncertainties and are based on the Company's current expectations and projections about future events that the Company believes may affect its financial condition, results of operations, business strategy, and financial needs. Investors can find many (but not all) of these statements by the use of words such as "approximates," "believes," "hopes," "expects," "anticipates," "estimates," "projects," "intends," "plans," "will," "would," "should," "could," "may," or other similar expressions in this prospectus. The Company undertakes no obligation to update or revise publicly any forward-looking statements to reflect subsequent occurring events or circumstances, or changes in its expectations, except as may be required by law. These statements are subject to uncertainties and risks, including, but not limited to, the uncertainties related to market conditions, and other factors discussed in the "Risk Factors" section of the registration statement filed with the SEC. Although the Company believes that the expectations expressed in these forward-looking statements are reasonable, it cannot assure you that such expectations will turn out to be correct, and the Company cautions investors that actual results may differ materially from the anticipated results and encourages investors to review other factors that may affect its future results in the registration statement and other filings with the SEC. Additional factors are discussed in the Company's filings with the SEC, which are available for review at www.sec.gov.

For more information, please contact:

D. Boral Capital LLC

Email: info@dboralcapital.com

Telephone: +1 (212) 970-5150

SOURCE: D. Boral Capital

data.title: SONIM TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Sonim Technologies Inc., a leading provider of enterprise 5G solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting its ongoing efforts to expand its product portfolio and market reach in the rugged mobile device industry.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenues: $11.2 million, a decrease of 3% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to a decline in sales of legacy phones.

Gross Profit: $0.8 million, a significant decrease of 72% compared to 2024, mainly attributed to the impairment of contract fulfillment assets and additional tariff costs.

Income (loss) from operations: $(6.7) million, representing a slight increase in loss by 4% compared to 2024.

Net income (loss): $(7.5) million, an increased loss by 13% compared to 2024.

Business Highlights

Company Overview: Sonim Technologies is a leading provider of enterprise 5G solutions, offering rugged handsets, smartphones, wireless internet devices, software, services, and accessories. The company serves sectors such as critical communications, first responders, government, industrial, construction, hospitality, and logistics.

Geographical Performance: Sonim's products are available through major carriers in the U.S., Canada, and Australia, and are expanding in Europe and South Africa through new distribution partners. This expansion supports the company's commitment to offering reliable solutions and expanding its customer base.

New Product Launches: In the second half of 2024 and through the filing date of this report, Sonim launched several new products, including the Sonim H500-series of 5G mobile hotspots, Sonim MegaConnect, Sonim H100 4G mobile hotspot, XP100 4G and XP400 5G professional rugged phones, XP Pro 5G rugged smartphone, XP Pro Thermal 5G rugged smartphone, and XP3plus 5G rugged flip phone.

Product Features: The new products boast features such as IP ratings, MIL-STD-810H standards, and elements of Sonim’s RPS, highlighting the company's value proposition to target markets.

Future Outlook: Sonim is focused on expanding its product portfolio throughout 2025, with plans to launch the XP Pro Thermal 5G smartphone in Europe, featuring an SDK-enabled Sonim IRIS software and an integrated thermal camera by FLIR®.

Revenue Segments: For the six months ended June 30, 2025, rugged smartphones represented 57% of total net revenues, feature phones were 31%, and connected solutions were 11%. For the three months ended June 30, 2025, rugged smartphones represented 43% of total net revenues, feature phones were 34%, and connected solutions were 20%.

Customer Base: The company's customer base is highly concentrated, with wireless carriers contributing 86% of total net revenues for the six months ended June 30, 2025, and the top three carrier customers accounting for 74% of total net revenues.

Trade Agreements Act Compliance: Sonim has prioritized its Trade Agreements Act initiatives to enhance market strategy and expand opportunities within government and enterprise markets, reinforcing its position as a trusted provider of enterprise 5G solutions.

SEC Filing: SONIM TECHNOLOGIES INC [ SONM ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: RAYONIER INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Rayonier Inc., a leading timberland real estate investment trust (REIT), has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by strategic divestitures and a focus on core operations in the Southern and Pacific Northwest regions.

Financial Highlights

Rayonier Inc. reported robust financial results for the three months ended June 30, 2025, showcasing substantial growth across key metrics:

  • Sales: $106.5 million, up from $99.6 million in the same period of 2024.
  • Operating Income: $14.5 million, a significant increase from $4.5 million in the same period of 2024, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $413.6 million, a dramatic rise from $3.0 million in the same period of 2024, primarily due to the gain on the sale of discontinued operations.
  • Net Income Attributable to Rayonier Inc.: $408.7 million, compared to $1.9 million in the same period of 2024.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share Attributable to Rayonier Inc.: $2.63, compared to $0.01 in the same period of 2024.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share Attributable to Rayonier Inc.: $2.63, compared to $0.01 in the same period of 2024.

Business Highlights

Rayonier Inc. also reported notable operational achievements and strategic shifts during the second quarter of 2025:

  • Revenue Segments: The company generated significant revenue from its Southern Timber and Pacific Northwest Timber segments. Southern Timber contributed $43.8 million, while Pacific Northwest Timber added $21.2 million to the total timber sales for the three months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Geographical Performance: The company completed the sale of its New Zealand operations, which were previously a significant part of its geographical revenue. These operations are now classified as discontinued, impacting the geographical revenue distribution.
  • Sales Units: The Southern Timber segment reported sales of 19,132 units under Stumpage Pay-as-Cut contracts and 24,631 units under Delivered Wood (Domestic) contracts. The Pacific Northwest Timber segment reported 561 units under Stumpage Lump Sum contracts and 20,601 units under Delivered Wood (Domestic) contracts for the three months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Following the divestiture of its New Zealand operations, Rayonier Inc. is focusing on optimizing its core operations in the Southern and Pacific Northwest regions. This strategic shift is expected to streamline operations and enhance focus on domestic markets.

SEC Filing: RAYONIER INC [ RYN ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025
